name: Grand Est (France)

colors.symbolism: the silver field of the historic Champagne bend
the gold (or) of the Lorraine field and of the alerions of Lorraine
the gules field of Alsace and the bend of Champagne

description: Three vertical panels from hoist to fly: a white panel with the bend of Champagne, a gold panel with the red bend and silver alerions of Lorraine, and a red panel with the white bend and crowns of Alsace.
history: Grand Est is a French region created by the territorial reform of 2016, merging Champagne-Ardenne, Lorraine, and Alsace. France traditionally does not promote regional flags, and this composite tri-banner is used in a de-facto capacity rather than as an officially adopted regional flag.

features.symbolism: Three vertical panels of unequal width displaying, from hoist to fly, the historic armorial banners of Champagne-Ardenne, Lorraine, and Alsace - the three former regions merged in 2016 to form Grand Est.

features.role: the historic armorial banners of Champagne-Ardenne (hoist), Lorraine (centre), and Alsace (fly)
features.symbolism: Each panel displays the heraldic banner of one of the historic provinces brought together by the 2016 territorial reform, with Champagne's bend cotised, Lorraine's red bend with three silver alerions, and Alsace's bend with white crowns on a red field.
